topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with the sterile finality of a death sentence. My carefully crafted piece, the one I poured three months of sleepless nights into, was credited to ‘Alex Thorne.’ I stared at the screen, a slow, cold burn starting low in my stomach. Alex Thorne. Some slick-haired, mediocre hack who clearly skimmed the surface of my work and slapped his name on it for a quick boost. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight, and let out a breath that tasted like ash. I didn't even bother replying. The only appropriate response was to delete everything, to burn the manuscript, and to watch Thorne’s little career sputter out.

[story 2]

When I saw the attribution, the air seemed to thin out in the room. My hands clenched so tightly my knuckles turned white, the pressure building until a dull ache throbbed up my forearms. It wasn't just a mistake; it was a deliberate slight, a casual theft of my intellectual property. I picked up the printed version of the article, the glossy paper feeling suddenly heavy and insulting in my grip. I traced the unfamiliar signature across the page with a fingertip, a small, tight smile playing on my lips. I stood up, walked to the window, and watched the city lights blur—all those glittering distractions seemed utterly insignificant compared to the cheapness of the deception I’d just uncovered.

[story 3]

I read the publication notice twice, my jaw tightening until my teeth ached. The realization hit me not as a shock, but as a profound settling of dust—the realization that someone else had been allowed to polish my creation. A slow, deliberate movement took over me. I picked up my phone, not to call an editor, but to draft a scathing, perfectly worded rebuttal. My voice, when I finally spoke into the phone, was low, almost unnervingly calm. "It seems," I said, the words dripping with practiced disdain, "that some people confuse plagiarism with authorship." I hung up before they could respond, the silence in the apartment now thick with self-satisfaction.
